reviews.author: Queenie O.
reviews.text: Vermicelli roll is the sleeper hit. Light, fresh, perfectly steamed.

reviews.author: Queenie O.
reviews.text: Baked char siu bao is the best dim sum item on earth. I will not be taking questions.

reviews.author: Amy W.
reviews.text: Cheung fun is the move. Shrimp, peanut sauce, perfect temperature.

reviews.author: Brian N.

reviews.author: Jenny Z.
reviews.text: Better than the average dim sum place. Worse than the family-run spots in Kowloon.

reviews.author: Brian N.
reviews.text: I grew up eating dim sum in Hong Kong. Tim Ho Wan is what I want it to taste like.

reviews.author: Jenny Z.
reviews.text: Egg yolk custard bun is what dreams are made of.

reviews.author: Eric L.
reviews.text: Forty dollars for a Michelin-starred meal. We are not in Paris anymore.

reviews.author: Vivian C.
reviews.text: Came at 7am on a weekday. Got a table in 10 minutes. Worth setting the alarm.

reviews.author: Linda T.
reviews.text: It is dim sum. Some of it is great. Some is fine. That is dim sum.
